figures.caption: Figure 2: Posterior skill λi per model, ranked by posterior mean. Thick lines are 50% credible intervals; thin lines are 95%. The dotted reference line marks the prior mean (λ = 1).

Table 3: Same-provider preference regression coefficients. Panel A is the pooled OLS; Panel B reports per-provider interaction coefficients with finalist providers contributing fewer than 50 same-provider observations bundled into “other.”
Table 1: Model rankings from the Plackett–Luce posterior. Mean is the posterior mean of λi. 95% CI is from posterior quantiles. Sampler: T = 2000 iterations, B = 500 burn-in, seed 42.
Table 2: Head-to-head pairwise statistics for the focal models.

Figure 3: Same-provider voting preference. Left: pooled effect of same_provider on Pr(voted for finalist) with 95% CI. Right: per-finalist-provider interaction effects from voted_for ∼ same_provider : provider + provider. Providers with fewer than 50 same-provider observations are bundled into “other.” SE clustered by game.

Figure 1: Pairwise head-to-head statistics for the five focal models. Left: Cliff’s δ — posterior epistemic confidence in skill ordering, valued in [−1, 1]. Right: Plackett–Luce predictive head-tohead win rate E[λA/(λA + λB)] ∈ [0, 1]. Cells read as “A vs B” (row vs column).
